title: Give related resources one shared spine
statement: Related resource types should share a layout, taxonomy, and component spine, varying only where type, ownership, or state creates a real difference.
rationale: Shared structure improves learnability and prevents sibling resource systems from drifting into separate products.

prefer: Reuse the same information skeleton and shared components.
avoid: Independent layouts, status vocabularies, and row structures for sibling resource types.

when: Several resource types live in one product hub or lifecycle.
not_when: A resource has a fundamentally different user job or lifecycle.

evidence.summary: Asked detail pages and resource rows to share taxonomy, layout, and components rather than invent parallel structures.
Requested a reusable detail-page taxonomy based on what each resource's metadata means.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Reuse the same information skeleton and shared components.
negative_examples: Independent layouts, status vocabularies, and row structures for sibling resource types.

checks: Do sibling resource pages share the same structural grammar?
Are differences explained by real resource semantics?
Can one shared component own the common behavior?
